$comment: Machine-readable agent capability manifest for PalLLM. Companion to AGENTS.md (the human-readable briefing). Designed to be loaded by an AI coding agent (or any caller) at session start so the agent knows in one structured payload what this repo is, what entry points exist, what the hard rules are, and how to verify a change. Drift-gated: scripts/run_full_audit.ps1 verifies that every path mentioned here actually exists and that the counts match the live code. Schema validation: this file conforms to docs/schemas/agents.schema.json (JSON Schema 2020-12).

project.name: PalLLM
project.scope: Palworld + UE4SS native bridge; portable adapter surface usable from any game / app

rootEntryPoints.audience: incoming coding agent picking up an interrupted session
humans + agents looking for any doc
shell-driven workflows; the canonical command surface
agents that want a machine-readable verb table without parsing PowerShell

rollingState.$comment: Cross-checked against PROJECT_NUMBERS.json + the live code by drift gates. Bump in lockstep with any code change.

capabilities.$comment: Each entry is a capability an agent can exercise in this repo. Cross-referenced from docs/HARVEST.md.

verbRunner.summary: Single-command access to every common operation via pal.ps1. The discovery surface for agents and humans alike.

httpSurface.summary: 57 /api/* routes + 6 operational routes + 1 /mcp route. Self-documenting via /openapi/v1.json and the in-tree snapshot.

mcpSurface.summary: MCP-over-HTTP server exposing 38 tools, 6 resources + 1 templated resource, 4 prompts.

deterministicFallback.summary: 19 hand-authored strategies that always return a coherent reply. Fires when inference is disabled / down / rate-limited.

inferenceWiring.summary: One-command path from clean clone to a working LLM endpoint. Nine connectors as of Pass 357; bundled llama.cpp is the default for reference-rig hardware; cloud API is the shipping escape path for below-reference rigs.

diagnostics.summary: Sidecar health, smoke loop, delivery replay, support bundle - all behind verbs.

harvesting.summary: Lift any single capability into another project without taking the rest of the repo.

validationGates.$comment: All 16 must pass for a change to ship. Same set as scripts/run_full_audit.ps1.

buildAndTest.fixHint: Inspect the build output; this codebase ships at 0 warnings, every warning is a regression.
Failing tests describe the broken invariant; the test name is the contract.

drift.what: No mojibake byte sequences in source or docs.
README + ROADMAP + ARCHITECTURE + API + CODE_MAP + HANDOFF agree with Program.cs api.Map* count.
API.md cross-references match code (operational + protocol routes counted separately).
Live build's OpenAPI matches the committed snapshot at docs/openapi/palllm-sidecar-v1.json.
Feature catalog count matches across docs and PalLlmFeatureCatalog.cs.
Ready / scaffolded / deferred split matches across docs.
Fallback strategy count in ROADMAP matches Try_* methods in FallbackBehaviorEngine.cs.
Test count in docs matches code.
Release-facing copy is neutral; no third-party brand names in INDEX.md / README.md / RELEASE.md.
Every repo-local path mentioned in docs / scripts / source actually exists on disk.
agents.json conforms to docs/schemas/agents.schema.json (required keys, types, shapes).
Every audited doc has a Last audited stamp <= 45 days old.
Hot-file line counts in prose mirrors (CLAUDE.md, CHEAT_SHEET.md, copilot-instructions.md, ANTI_PATTERNS.md, HARVEST.md) stay within 5% of the actual wc -l value.

hardRules: Deterministic fallback always answers. POST /api/chat MUST never return a 500 because inference is off.
Default install is fully local. Zero outbound network unless the operator explicitly configures live inference / vision / TTS / OTLP / update-check endpoints.
Every drift gate stays green on every change.
Feature catalog entries are an explicit contract. Remove only with status:deprecated + CHANGELOG note.
API errors use ProblemDetails. No stack traces, no exception type names in /api/* response bodies.
Packaged EXE boot path is sacred. If you touch static asset handling, test against sidecar/publish/PalLLM.Sidecar.exe, not just dotnet run.
Docs and code travel together. If you change a number in the code, bump it in PROJECT_NUMBERS.json AND the docs that pin it AND the drift gate that verifies it - in the same commit.

antiPatterns.summary: Things that look like good ideas but have already been considered and rejected here.

agentHelpers.$comment: Verbs designed specifically for agent use - every one of these returns structured (machine-readable) output.

context.what: Single-shot JSON snapshot: counts + ADR titles + schema paths + freshness. Agent context warmup.

explain.what: Structured explanation of any file: kind, public surface, dependents, dependencies, related docs, related tests.

where.what: Natural-language query -> ranked file paths. 'where do MCP tools live?' or 'where is the chat hot path?'

scaffold.what: Generate placeholder files for a new advisor / builder / validator / feeder / fallback / mcp-tool following the canonical patterns.

harvestableUnits.$comment: Independently liftable capabilities. Each one is documented + tested + has a clear public surface.

honestVerdict.$comment: From docs/READINESS.md - 23 user-facing aspects scored candidly. Aggregate is computed, not aspirational.

topGap.reason: Phase 4 of docs/ROADMAP.md - requires live Palworld + UE4SS session; cannot be moved by a coding agent alone.
